rendered_lines: AIA possesses multiple distinctive competitive advantages that set us apart in a highly competitive market. With decades of operations
across Asia, we have an unparalleled platform in the region and hold leading market positions in most of the markets where we operate.

Our unwavering 100% focus on the Asian market enables us to fully capitalize on growth opportunities in the world's most attractive life insurance region. This focus, combined with our leadership team's extensive experience in Asia and our employees' exceptional capabilities,

rendered_lines: Our unparalleled distribution strength enables us to reach customers directly and provide high-quality professional advice to help meet
the substantial and growing long-term protection and savings needs of Asian consumers. Today, the public increasingly expects

companies to address their needs in ways that are consistent with long-term sustainable development. AIA possesses a market-leading brand, built on the foundation of helping people achieve their goals of living healthier, longer and better lives.

rendered_lines: Despite 2020 being unprecedented, I am more optimistic than ever about the life insurance industry prospects in Asia and AIA's future.
We continue to challenge ourselves and develop strategic priorities to meet evolving customer expectations and financial needs. Our
new corporate strategy builds on AIA's existing strengths and advances our transformation to deliver superior service to customers.

rendered_lines: 2020 Annual Report 011
The pandemic's principal impact on our 2020 financial performance was on new business sales, as containment measures across many
markets restricted face-to-face sales activities, particularly in H1 2020. Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased 33%
versus 2019, we rapidly adopted digital tools, and widespread relaxation of movement restrictions supported sales momentum recovery. AIA China

rendered_by_block.p19_b1: The pandemic's principal impact on our 2020 financial performance was on new business sales, as containment measures across many
rendered_by_block.p19_b2: markets restricted face-to-face sales activities, particularly in H1 2020. Although VONB of US$2.765 billion in 2020 decreased 33%
rendered_by_block.p19_b3: versus 2019, we rapidly adopted digital tools, and widespread relaxation of movement restrictions supported sales momentum recovery. AIA China

rendered_lines: contributed the largest share to the Group's VONB and was also our first reporting segment to demonstrate a recovery in sales momentum, as this region began to gradually
ease strict lockdowns from March 2020 onwards. Excluding sales from Mainland Chinese visitors, which remained at low levels, the Group's other segments showed strong
recovery in VONB in the second half, growing 23% compared to the first half. The Group's new business momentum remained robust into 2021, with VONB in the first two
months 15% higher than the corresponding period in 2020.

rendered_lines: This was due to EV operating profit of US$7.243 billion offsetting lower government bond yields. EV operating profit included positive
operating variances of US$549 million from favorable experience versus EV assumptions.

rendered_by_block.p19_b10: This was due to EV operating profit of US$7.243 billion offsetting lower government bond yields. EV operating profit included positive
rendered_by_block.p19_b11: operating variances of US$549 million from favorable experience versus EV assumptions.

rendered_lines: Our high-quality, recurring earnings sources and active management of our in-force portfolio drove OPAT growth of 5% to US$5.942 billion, while
maintaining an operating margin of 16.9%. Renewal premiums collected increased by 10%, with recurring premiums representing over 90% of total
premiums collected in 2020. OPAT growth benefited from significant positive claims experience throughout the year, which offset the decline in yields on
new fixed income investments and the impact of reduced assumed long-term equity investment returns. As expected, the exceptional positive medical
claims experience noted in the first half did not recur in the second half, but we continued to see positive contributions to OPAT growth from this source.
With AIA's Thailand business lapse experience normalizing, persistency improved in the second half, with the Group's persistency at 95%.

expected investment returns. As at 31 December 2020, the Group's financial position remained strong, with Free Surplus of US$13.473
billion after dividend payments of US$1.997 billion.

rendered_lines: As at 31 December 2020, our principal operating company AIA Co.'s solvency ratio remained very strong at 489%. As at 31 December
2020, under the new Group regulatory framework, the Group LCSM cover ratio was 374%. Going forward, we will disclose the Group
LCSM cover ratio as the primary measure of the Group's regulatory solvency position.

rendered_lines: The Board recommends an increase in the final dividend of 7.5% to 100.30 Hong Kong cents per share, subject to approval by
shareholders at the Company's forthcoming Annual General Meeting. This brings the total dividend for 2020 to 135.30 Hong Kong cents

per share, representing an increase of 6.9% compared to the total dividend for 2019. This reflects AIA's long-established prudent, sustainable and progressive dividend policy, which enables the Group to capitalize on future growth opportunities while maintaining financial flexibility.

rendered_lines: We remain confident in AIA's business opportunities across Asia. We will continue deploying capital to achieve profitable new business
growth, leveraging competitive advantages while maintaining prudent financial management and delivering long-term shareholder value.

rendered_lines: Although VONB of US$2,765 million in 2020 decreased 33% from 2019, swift adoption of digital tools and widespread relaxation of
travel restrictions supported sales momentum recovery. AIA China contributed most to Group VONB in 2020 and was the first segment
demonstrating recovery, as the region progressively relaxed lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding subdued mainland Chinese visitor
sales to Hong Kong, other segments' VONB rebounded strongly in H2, growing 23% versus H1. Group new business momentum
remained robust in 2021, with VONB in the first two months up 15% versus the same period in 2020.

rendered_by_block.p20_b21: Although VONB of US$2,765 million in 2020 decreased 33% from 2019, swift adoption of digital tools and widespread relaxation of
rendered_by_block.p20_b22: travel restrictions supported sales momentum recovery. AIA China contributed most to Group VONB in 2020 and was the first segment
rendered_by_block.p20_b23: demonstrating recovery, as the region progressively relaxed lockdowns from March 2020. Excluding subdued mainland Chinese visitor

rendered_lines: AIA China achieved strong momentum in 2021, with VONB in the first two months recording very robust growth versus 2020. VONB of
US$968 million in 2020 decreased 17% from 2019, primarily due to limited sales during the early COVID-19 outbreak in Q1. As travel
restrictions eased, new business momentum improved rapidly. VONB resumed customary seasonal patterns with greater concentration
in H1. In 2020, AIA China contributed the largest share to Group VONB.

rendered_by_block.p20_b26: AIA China achieved strong momentum in 2021, with VONB in the first two months recording very robust growth versus 2020. VONB of

rendered_lines: Following travel restrictions, new business sales from Mainland Chinese visitors to AIA Hong Kong were effectively suspended from
early February 2020, causing significant VONB decline. VONB margin decreased due to reduced new business volume, underwriting
expense overruns, product mix changes, and lower interest rates. Macau resumed the Individual Visit Scheme for Mainland Chinese
visitors in late September 2020, supporting new business sales recovery from Mainland visitors, which exceeded one-third of AIA Macau's annualized new premiums in Q4.
